title: Error using X-assign-flatParam-prior with freely floating pdf parameters

posts.name: Federica Simone
posts.text:
Dear experts,
we use the  “Hybrid-Bayesian” method for setting upper limits using Combine v10.0.2. We do a shape analysis on the invariant mass, where the background is modeled by an exponential function whose slope is left freely floating in the fit.
Therefore, the slope of the exponential is set as a flatParam in the datacard.
When running
text2workspace.py datacard.txt -o workspace.root --X-assign-flatParam-prior
we get the error below [*].
Could you please point us to the correct procedure in this case, i.e. using “Hybrid-Bayesian” while keeping some pdf parameters freely floating?
Best,
Federica for the BPH-24-010 team
@cbasile
[*]
Traceback (most recent call last):
File "/lustrehome/fsimone/Tau3muRun3/Combine/CMSSW_14_1_0_pre4/bin/slc7_amd64_gcc12/text2workspace.py", line 104, in <module>
MB.doModel(justCheckPhysicsModel=options.justCheckPhysicsModel)
File "/lustrehome/fsimone/Tau3muRun3/Combine/CMSSW_14_1_0_pre4/src/HiggsAnalysis/CombinedLimit/python/ModelTools.py", line 183, in doModel
self.doAutoFlatNuisancePriors()
File "/lustrehome/fsimone/Tau3muRun3/Combine/CMSSW_14_1_0_pre4/src/HiggsAnalysis/CombinedLimit/python/ModelTools.py", line 835, in doAutoFlatNuisancePriors
v, x1, x2 = self.out.var(c_param_name).getVal(), self.out.var(c_param_name).getMin(), self.out.var(c_param_name).getMax()
File "/cvmfs/cms.cern.ch/slc7_amd64_gcc12/lcg/root/6.30.07-f3322c77db1c59847b28fde88ff7218c/lib/ROOT/_pythonization/_roofit/_rooabsreal.py", line 131, in getVal
return self._getVal(normalizationSet) if normalizationSet else self._getVal()
TypeError: none of the 2 overloaded methods succeeded. Full details:
double RooAbsReal::getVal(const RooArgSet& normalisationSet) =>
TypeError: takes at least 1 arguments (0 given)
attempt to access a null-pointer

Dear experts,

we use the “Hybrid-Bayesian” method for setting upper limits using Combine v10.0.2. We do a shape analysis on the invariant mass, where the background is modeled by an exponential function whose slope is left freely floating in the fit.
Therefore, the slope of the exponential is set as a flatParam in the datacard.

When running
text2workspace.py datacard.txt -o workspace.root --X-assign-flatParam-prior
we get the error below [*].
Could you please point us to the correct procedure in this case, i.e. using “Hybrid-Bayesian” while keeping some pdf parameters freely floating?

Best,
Federica for the BPH-24-010 team

posts.name: Nicholas Wardle
posts.text:
Hi Federica,
This seems to be an issue in that the parameter you are attaching a flat prior too is one that text2workspace doesn’t know about (yet). basically, we have a null pointer in this block of code : https://github.com/cms-analysis/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/blob/main/python/ModelTools.py#L834-L843
Note that this doesn’t happen for parameters like normalisations as they can be explicitly present before this code is executed.
I would need to think a bit about how to fix it, unless another expert spots an obvious solution.
Best,
Nick
posts.cooked_html:
Hi Federica,

This seems to be an issue in that the parameter you are attaching a flat prior too is one that text2workspace doesn’t know about (yet). basically, we have a null pointer in this block of code : https://github.com/cms-analysis/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/blob/main/python/ModelTools.py#L834-L843

Note that this doesn’t happen for parameters like normalisations as they can be explicitly present before this code is executed.

I would need to think a bit about how to fix it, unless another expert spots an obvious solution.

Best,
Nick

posts.name: Nicholas Wardle
posts.text:
Hi again,
I thought a bit more and found a workaround. Can you try this PR  https://github.com/cms-analysis/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/pull/1040  ?
All you need to do in your datacard is to extend the declaration of the flatParam to include a default value (its not very important of course for this) and the range for the prior).  eg,
name flatPrior x [xmin,xmax]
Can you try that and let me know if it works?
Best,
Nick
posts.cooked_html:
Hi again,

I thought a bit more and found a workaround. Can you try this PR https://github.com/cms-analysis/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit/pull/1040 ?

All you need to do in your datacard is to extend the declaration of the flatParam to include a default value (its not very important of course for this) and the range for the prior). eg,

name flatPrior x [xmin,xmax]

Can you try that and let me know if it works?

Best,
Nick

posts.name: Federica Simone
posts.text:
Hi Nick,
thank you very much for the prompt feedback. I did the following:

take the PR and compile

-bash-4.2$ git remote -v
origin	https://github.com/cms-analysis/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit.git (fetch)
origin	https://github.com/cms-analysis/HiggsAnalysis-CombinedLimit.git (push)
-bash-4.2$ git pull origin pull/1040/head

Test with the old datacard. I now getting the new warning:

ERROR in flatParam - you need to include nominal value (v) and range  - name flatParam v [x1,x2] when including flat prior for parameter bkg_exp_slope_threeGlobal_2023_A1

Modify my datacard accordingly:

bkg_exp_slope_threeGlobal_2023_A1 flatParam -1. [-1000.,100.]

but the same error is raised:
ERROR in flatParam - you need to include nominal value (v) and range  - name flatParam v [x1,x2] when including flat prior for parameter bkg_exp_slope_threeGlobal_2023_A1

is the syntax wrong?
Thank you and best,
Federica

  • Test with the old datacard. I now getting the new warning:

  • Modify my datacard accordingly:
bkg_exp_slope_threeGlobal_2023_A1 flatParam -1. [-1000.,100.]

but the same error is raised:

ERROR in flatParam - you need to include nominal value (v) and range  - name flatParam v [x1,x2] when including flat prior for parameter bkg_exp_slope_threeGlobal_2023_A1

is the syntax wrong?

Thank you and best,
Federica

posts.name: Nicholas Wardle
posts.text:
Hi Federica,
Nope, my mistake - I forgot to also push a necessary edit for DatacardParser.py. I have pushed it now so hopefully if you pull again and try it will be ok
Best,
Nick

sorry for the late answer. It works now, thank you!

Federica
